findings.description: Header layout is broken, duplicated, and mixed with footer elements.
findings.evidence: The HTML duplicates the 'PAT McCRORY...' header text on the left side and places the footer text and colored bar above the main document title 'MEMORANDUM'.

findings.description: Footer content is incorrectly inserted into the document reading flow.
findings.evidence: The footer text and colored bar appear at the top of the content area, before the 'I. History/Background' section heading.

findings.description: Footer content breaks a sentence and bullet point in half.
findings.evidence: The footer text and colored bar are injected between 'Coal ash should be' and 'disposed of on Duke Energy property...' within the 'Environmental' bullet point.

findings.description: Footer content interrupts the logical flow between related paragraphs.
findings.evidence: The footer text and colored bar appear between the Section III introductory paragraph and the subsequent sentence 'The following is a summary...'.

findings.description: Footer content is inappropriately placed between sections.
findings.evidence: The footer text and colored bar appear directly before the 'IV. Attachments' heading.

findings.description: Pagination artifacts interrupt a continuous sentence across a page break.
findings.evidence: In the HTML, the transition from Page 2 to Page 3 occurs in the middle of a sentence inside a list item. The text reads '...Coal ash should be' followed immediately by a `<div class="page-footer">` and a `<figure>`, before concluding with 'disposed of on Duke Energy property...' on the other side. While no text is lost, this disrupts the structural integrity of the sentence and the list item.
